title: Simmer's A Pleasant Time.
content:
Tune - "Ay waukin o'."


I.

        Simmer's a pleasant time,
            Flow'rs of ev'ry colour;
        The water rins o'er the heugh,
            And I long for my true lover.
                    Ay waukin O,
                        Waukin still and wearie:
                    Sleep I can get nane
                        For thinking on my dearie.

II.

        When I sleep I dream,
            When I wauk I'm eerie;
        Sleep I can get nane
            For thinking on my dearie.

III.

        Lanely night comes on,
            A' the lave are sleeping;
        I think on my bonnie lad
            And I bleer my een with greetin'.
                    Ay waukin O,
                        Waukin still and wearie:
                    Sleep I can get nane
                        For thinking on my dearie.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Simmer's A Pleasant Time.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
